_comment: Declarative workflow definition for meeting-classifier. Classifies a meeting from available evidence clues, attaches IEP/504/medical advisories, and routes to the appropriate TOS skill. It classifies and routes — it does NOT author meeting artifacts.
description: Meeting classification: gather clues → classify type + intent → attach advisories → route to owning skill → quality gate

steps.note: Collect evidence per references/evidence-model.md: explicit statement, calendar event, sender title, attendee roles, subject/body, prior thread, transcript. Strong clues override weak ones.
Classify meeting type and request intent. When two types are materially plausible, emit a MINORITY REPORT — do not guess.
IEP/504 meetings: attach required-cadence advisory (IDEA/Section 504 cite). Medical meetings: surface action plan from source on file (never fabricate). Flag safety-critical.

Route per references/meeting-taxonomy.md: parent contact → family-communication; IEP/504 → special-education-support; MTSS → intervention-mtss; observations → professional-learning / school-administration; planning → curriculum-mapping / lesson-planner.

note: meeting-classifier ROUTES — it does not author IEP/504 content, lesson plans, or letters. Never makes a determination about a student's plan, medical, or legal status — escalates those to a human/team.
